turns.user_message: I used to bounce back from a rough day so easily, but lately everything just sticks to me like glue. It has been dragging on for weeks now, and I catch myself staring at the ceiling wondering where that old spark went. Even small tasks feel heavier today, and I keep messing up simple things I used to breeze through. It’s frustrating because I know I’m capable, but my brain just won’t cooperate anymore. I just feel like a faded version of who I was back then.

It’s been months of this same heavy feeling, and I’m starting to accept that this is just how I am now. I constantly compare my current self to the person I used to be, and the gap just keeps widening every single day. I try to push through it, but my confidence keeps crumbling under the weight of my own doubts. Every little mistake feels like proof that I’m fundamentally losing my edge. I just hate watching myself shrink into someone who apologizes for taking up space.

I don’t even recognize myself anymore, and it’s exhausting pretending that I still have that old confidence tucked away somewhere. The contrast between who I was and this empty version of me feels completely unbearable. I keep waiting for a switch to flip back to normal, but the truth is I feel entirely broken inside. Every time I try to step up, my own mind immediately tears me down before I can even start. I’m just so tired of feeling like a complete disappointment to myself.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This has been going on for a while.
